data.title: Analyst Sees Resilient Membership Growth and Manageable Headwinds Supporting Planet Fitness Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Sharon Zackfia has reiterated their bullish stance on PLNT stock, giving a Buy rating on April 24.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sharon Zackfia’s rating is based on the view that Planet Fitness can deliver steady growth despite near-term headwinds, as evidenced by expectations for high single-digit revenue expansion and continued member gains even in a softer comp environment. She attributes temporary pressure to factors such as severe weather and the transition to click-to-cancel, but notes that these issues should ease as the company laps last year’s changes.

At the same time, she highlights ongoing membership growth, disciplined promotional activity that remains comparable year over year, and the ability to modestly grow earnings even with some margin compression from mix shifts like higher advertising revenue. In her assessment, this combination of resilient fundamentals and manageable near-term pressures supports a favorable long-term outlook for Planet Fitness stock, justifying a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 24, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $90.00 price target.

data.title: Bobby Brooks Reiterates Buy on Solaris Energy Infrastructure After Strong Q1 Beat, Contract Momentum; Keeps $81 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Solaris Energy Infrastructure, the Energy sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Bobby Brooks from Northland Securities maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $81.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bobby Brooks has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Solaris Energy Infrastructure’s stronger‑than‑expected first‑quarter performance and robust contract momentum. Revenue and adjusted EBITDA materially surpassed consensus, driven by higher managed megawatts, increased logistics deployments, and better revenue per megawatt, while segment profitability in both Power Solutions and Logistics exceeded forecasts, supporting confidence in cash generation.

Brooks also highlights the newly signed long‑term, investment‑grade 600 MW contract and year‑to‑date contracted volume of 1,100 MW, which still leaves meaningful open capacity and visibility on future growth. With upgraded second‑quarter guidance, an initial third‑quarter outlook, disciplined capital spending below prior expectations, and a solid project and acquisition pipeline, he sees continued upside for SEI shares and reiterates his $81 price target as unchanged.

Brooks covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Tetra Technologies, Graham, and Crane NXT. According to TipRanks, Brooks has an average return of 42.5% and a 71.95% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Morgan Stanley also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $81.00 price target.

data.description:

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Ben Pirie has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Sailfish’s stronger‑than‑expected Q4 performance and solid full‑year execution. The company delivered meaningful revenue growth, outpacing his forecasts, while maintaining roughly in‑line adjusted EBITDA and actively enhancing shareholder value through share repurchases.

He also highlights the attractive outlook for 2026, as the Mt. Hamilton stream is set to begin contributing cash flow and proceeds from the Spring Valley sale are redeployed into the portfolio. Combined with robust royalty income from assets such as Mako, these developments support his view that Sailfish offers compelling upside relative to his C$4.50 per share target price.

data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on ArriVent as Firmonertinib Data Boost Confidence; $42 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Analyst Sam Slutsky from LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on ArriVent BioPharma, Inc. and keeping the price target at $42.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sam Slutsky has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including supportive external validation for the Firmonertinib program and encouraging early efficacy data. The recent positive Phase 3 sunvozertinib result in first-line EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC versus chemotherapy reinforces the therapeutic relevance of this target class and strengthens confidence that ArriVent’s FURVENT Phase 3 trial could achieve a similar outcome.

In addition, Firmonertinib’s prior Phase 1b data show a high objective response rate and prolonged duration of response relative to historical benchmarks such as mobocertinib and amivantamab plus chemotherapy, despite cross-trial comparison caveats. The drug’s CNS penetrance and potentially cleaner tolerability profile versus sunvozertinib provide further differentiation, supporting the unchanged $42 price target and the view that AVBP shares offer attractive upside if Phase 3 results are successful.

In another report released on April 24, Citi also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $45.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Genmab: Buy Rating Backed by Expanded Petosemtamab-Keytruda Phase III Trial and Blockbuster Late-Stage Pipeline
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Phipps has maintained their bullish stance on GMAB stock, giving a Buy rating on April 22.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matt Phipps has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including the meaningful expansion of the Phase III petosemtamab plus Keytruda trial in frontline head and neck cancer, which signals confidence in the program’s potential and better alignment with peer studies in this setting. He also anticipates important clarity on the enrollment changes and patient demographics during the upcoming first-quarter earnings call, which could further validate the strategic direction of the trial.

In addition, Phipps highlights Genmab’s robust and growing royalty base from products like Darzalex, Kesimpta, Tecvayli, and Rybrevant, which supplies substantial funding for advancing its late-stage pipeline. Following the Merus acquisition, Genmab now controls three late-stage assets with blockbuster prospects, supporting a view that the company can sustain and accelerate revenue growth into the 2030s even after Darzalex royalties taper, with several clinical milestones anticipated in 2026 reinforcing his positive long-term outlook.

In another report released on April 22, Goldman Sachs also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a $30.50 price target.

data.title: Litigation Charge Weighs on Q1 GAAP Earnings but Leaves Valuation Intact as Analyst Reiterates Hold Rating and $40 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Tyler Van Buren from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on Moderna and keeping the price target at $40.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tyler Van Buren has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including the sizable one-time litigation payment Moderna made to Arbutus in the first quarter. He incorporates the $950 million charge into his cost of goods sold assumptions, which materially depresses reported GAAP earnings for Q1 but is not expected to affect the company’s cash position until the third quarter.

At the same time, this litigation-driven adjustment does not alter his broader valuation framework or long‑term cash flow outlook for Moderna. As a result, his discounted cash flow analysis continues to support an unchanged $40 price target, reinforcing a neutral stance in which the stock is viewed as fairly valued relative to its risk‑reward profile.

data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated as Strong VDPHL01 Data De-Risk Program and Drive Price Target Increase to $120
data.description:
Geoff Meacham, an analyst from Citi, maintained the Buy rating on Veradermics, Incorporated. The associated price target was raised to $120.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Geoff Meacham has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the strong Phase 2/3 results for VDPHL01 that showed best-in-class efficacy with a notably clean cardiac safety profile even in a largely hypertensive population. He views the limited adverse events and absence of serious cardiac issues as a major de-risking milestone for the program, supporting a substantial increase in modeled probability of success and raising the price target to $120.

Geoff Meacham’s rating is based on the significantly expanded commercial opportunity, with risk-adjusted peak sales now projected at $3.0B and further upside from female pattern hair loss and ex-U.S. markets that are not yet fully included in his forecasts. He also highlights strong validation from key opinion leaders, compelling pricing benchmarks, and a robust catalyst path including additional Phase 3 and extension data, all of which underpin his expectation of substantial share price appreciation and justify maintaining a Buy/High Risk stance.

data.title: Evaxion: Buy Rating Reiterated on AI Cancer Vaccine Platform and Upcoming Catalysts; $10 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Evaxion Biotech, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Debanjana Chatterjee from JonesTrading maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $10.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Debanjana Chatterjee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Evaxion’s AI-enabled cancer vaccine platform and upcoming catalysts. He views the AACR data for EVX-01 in first-line melanoma as demonstrating a highly differentiated profile, with strong, durable T-cell responses and a high rate of accurately selected neoantigens, suggesting best-in-class potential versus conventional approaches.

Chatterjee also highlights a series of near- and medium-term events that could significantly increase Evaxion’s strategic value, including pivotal external readouts in melanoma vaccines, Evaxion’s own 3‑year survival and immunogenicity update for EVX-01, and an expected IND filing for the off‑the‑shelf EVX‑04 AML program. Together with a manageable projected cash burn and the unchanged $10 price target, he sees an attractive risk‑reward skew and room for substantial upside if the data and sector tailwinds materialize as anticipated.

In another report released on April 24, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $16.00 price target.
